premise_with_term: The law says it is illegal to steal. (steal: take property without consent)
premise_different_meaning: Robin Hood stole from the rich to give to the poor. (stole: took property without consent but with moral justification)
conclusion: Therefore, Robin Hood acted illegally, and his actions should be condemned the same as any criminal theft.

premise_with_term: Nothing is better than world peace. (better: superior in value)
premise_different_meaning: A mediocre sandwich is better than nothing. (better: preferable to having nothing)
conclusion: Therefore, a mediocre sandwich is better than world peace.

premise_with_term: All men are mortal. (men: human beings)
premise_different_meaning: Socrates is a man. (man: adult male human)
conclusion: Therefore, if 'men' only referred to adult males, the first premise does not establish that all women or children are mortal.

premise_with_term: The study shows that moderate exercise improves mental health. (moderate: 150 minutes per week)
premise_different_meaning: The patient reported exercising moderately. (moderately: occasionally, about once per month)
conclusion: Therefore, the patient's exercise habits should be improving their mental health according to the study.

premise_with_term: We should trust peer-reviewed science. (trust: accept conclusions as well-supported by evidence)
premise_different_meaning: The peer-reviewed paper was later retracted. (peer-reviewed: passed initial review, now retracted)
conclusion: Therefore, because it was peer-reviewed, we must trust the retracted paper's conclusions.
